steps.title: Identify why tangent applies
steps.content:
The question asks for $\tan x^\circ$ in a right triangle. Tangent compares the side opposite the angle to the side adjacent to the angle:
$$\tan(\theta)=\dfrac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}}.$$

steps.title: Identify the two relevant sides
steps.content: From the figure, the side opposite $x^\circ$ is $26$, and the side adjacent to $x^\circ$ is $7$. Therefore, $$\tan(x^\circ)=\dfrac{26}{7}.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice C is correct. Choice B reverses or uses the wrong side relationship, and choice D adds side lengths instead of using opposite over adjacent.
